page: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is a high-performance computing instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture with Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 16 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 15200 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 121141.14 and a PassMark CPU score of 79173.37, both placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1692.0. The instance excels in memory bandwidth, achieving a top-tier read speed of 339.55 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 564.52 GB/sec and Redis SET operations, processing 117.53 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also highly efficient at 83.63 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hpc6id.32xlarge offers a dense aggregation of compute, memory, and high-speed local storage, making it highly effective for resource-intensive deployments. However, the lack of hyperthreading and average single-core speeds represent a tradeoff for workloads that do not scale horizontally or require high single-thread performance. This instance is optimized for tightly coupled high-performance computing workloads, large-scale database operations, memory-bound simulations, and data-intensive engineering tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 high-performance computing instance featuring 64 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 1024 GB of DDR4 RAM, 15200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 79173.37, and top-tier memory read bandwidth of 339.55 GB/sec. The instance also delivers exceptional throughput for Redis SET operations and multi-threaded decompression. While single-core performance is average, the high resource density and dedicated core allocation provide excellent qualitative value for parallelized, data-heavy tasks. The hpc6id.32xlarge is highly suited for high-performance computing, large-scale databases, and memory-bound engineering simulations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is a high-performance computing instance with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 15.2 TB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 1024 GB RAM, 15.2 TB NVMe SSD, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core high-performance computing instance featuring massive local NVMe storage, high memory bandwidth, and 200 Gbps networking.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores with 1 thread per core
1024 GB of DDR4 RAM and 15200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by AWS Nitro
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of 339.55 GB/sec
Optimized for high-performance computing and large-scale databases
